claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'd like to gather some information about your child and your circumstances.

**First, could you please tell me your child's name and age?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 17, I need to ask:

**Is Alex still in approved education or training?** (For example, studying at school, college, or in approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They left school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I need to understand Alex's current situation a bit more:

**Has Alex started paid work? If so, how many hours per week are they working?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't started working. They're not in any paid employment at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to check one more thing about Alex's situation:

**Is Alex claiming any benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't claiming any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **unfortunately you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

Here's why:

For children aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can continue if the child is under 20 **and** stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 17 and is not currently in approved education or training, this means you don't meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

**However, there may be options:**

- If Alex were to register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services after leaving education, you could potentially apply for Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks.

- If Alex returns to approved education or training in the future, you would become eligible to claim Child Benefit again at that point.

I know this isn't the answer you were hoping for, but I hope this information has been helpful in understanding your current situation.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has left education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 17 but is not in approved education or training, and is not working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits (which would also make them ineligible), the key issue is that Alex does not meet the requirement of being in approved education or training. Without being enrolled in approved education or training, a 16 or 17 year old does not qualify their parent/guardian for Child Benefit, even if they live at home and are not working. There is a provision for continuing Child Benefit for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but there's no indication this applies here.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who lives with them in the UK. While the claimant meets the basic residency requirements and no one else is claiming for Alex, Alex does not meet the eligibility criteria for children aged 16 or over because Alex is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds requires the child to be in approved education or training, which is not the case here.

Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds requires the child to be in approved education or training, which is not the case here.
